observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management undertake all procedures prescribed in COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to come up with accurate and reliable balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor undertake the following courses of action:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary journal entries to correct the balances of the “Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses” account and the affected permanent accounts; and
Advise the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all transactions are recorded in the correct accounts in the general ledger in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended that the Management undertake the following courses of action:
The Municipal Accountant draw the adjusting journal entry to restore the cash and liabilities pertaining to the stale checks to present fairly the recorded balance of the Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts, and shall adjust its books regularly and promptly whenever checks issued become stale;
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer coordinate in the monitoring of the status of all approved checks on a regular basis; and
The Municipal Treasurer develop internal rules and procedures in ensuring that all approved checks will be released promptly to payees to avoid or decrease the existence of stale checks due to unclaimed checks.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the subsidiary ledgers of the individual taxpayers with the general ledger balances of the RPT and SET Receivables.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to locate the missing liquidation reports and submit the same to settle this liability, and ensure reconciliation of the LGU’s accounts with 
DA-BSWM pursuant to Section 111 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately prepare an adjusting entry to record the balance of the financial assistance amounting to P7,953,926.00 to the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account. Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the accounting and reporting guidelines on the receipt and utilization of donations for DRRM programs pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Management ensure the transfer of all unexpended/unobligated balances of the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE at year-end to the Special Trust Fund under the account “Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund” (account number 2-04-01-020) in the Trust Fund book.

We recommended the following to the Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to trace and verify the cause of the previous year DRRM adjustment in the intra-agency accounts, and effect the necessary adjustment; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to regularly reconcile the accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, to ensure correct account balances and present reliable data pursuant to Section 112 of Presidential Decree 1445 and Paragraph 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the required documents supporting the thirty-six (36) transactions, and strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on the submission of accounts and other reports of the LGU to avoid administrative sanctions and/or disallowance of said accounts as provided for in Section 39 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive undertake the following courses of action:
Require the Disbursing Officer to discontinue the payment of financial assistance through petty cash fund, and ensure that the said fund should only be used for cash payment of non-recurring, emergency and petty expenses of the LGU, which could not be conveniently paid in checks or which require immediate payment in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Furthermore, we recommended that the Disbursing Officer strictly observe that each petty cash voucher should not exceed P1,000.00, in accordance with Section 48 of the New Government Accounting System; and
Enjoin the concerned department/s to prepare written guidelines or policies and procedures on the payment of burial and medical assistance taking into consideration the process of assessment, approval, and release of payment and the amount that the constituent may avail.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive, undertake the following courses of action:
Require the Municipal Development Council to carefully plan and analyze the projects, programs and activities under the 20% Development Fund such that details of the identified priority projects under the said fund are specified, including project location, specific implementation schedule, and physical target, and avoid generic or lumpsum projects;
Stop the practice of implementing generic or lumpsum projects without further authority from the Sanggunian to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit; and
Comply strictly with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular 
No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 by ensuring that all charges against the 20% Development Fund are not among those prohibited in the JMC, otherwise, the transactions shall be suspended/disallowed in audit.

We recommended the following to the Management:
The Municipal Budget Officer to strictly monitor the utilization of appropriation of all programs, projects and activities of the LGU, and comply with Sections 336 and 344 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 4 (1) of Presidential Decree No. 1445; and
The concerned officials to submit an explanation on the reason behind the disbursement of funds beyond the appropriated amount amounting to P473,955.00, and refund the same.

We recommended that the Management, through the Local Chief Executive, being the Chairman of the GAD Focal Point System, undertake the following courses of action:
Justify/explain the inappropriate use of GAD Fund totaling P1,871,054.52 and the excessive disbursement on the Family and Health Care Services Program to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit;
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly monitor the utilization of appropriation of all GAD programs, projects and activities of the LGU, and comply with Section 336 of the Local Government Code of 1991; and
Ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD PPAs and the judicious utilization of the GAD budget, and comply strictly with Section 35 of the General Appropriations Act of 2024 and Item 2.5 of PCW-NEDA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01 by ensuring that the performance outcomes/indicators of all charges in GAD Fund are linked to the approved Annual GAD Plan and Budget, otherwise, the transactions may be suspended/disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Management, thru the Local Chief Executive, prepare a 5-Year Business Plan for each LEE and conduct a Semi-Annual Financial Self-Sufficiency Review to ensure that each LEE is operating towards self-sufficiency or 100 percent cost-recovery and according to the Business Plan.

We recommended that the Management create a plan to improve the Local Economic Enterprises’ financial performance in accordance with the Manual on the setting up and operation of Local Economic Enterprises and install measures to mitigate further losses on its operation.

We recommended that the Management reinforce Executive Order No. 041 s. 2024 dated November 5, 2024, and monitor the progress and execution of approved activities in order to increase the LGU’s tax collection, especially on Business Tax and RPT delinquent accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor undertake the following courses of action:
Adopt policies and internal control measures to monitor loan accommodations and other payroll deductions, such that the monthly net take home pay of an employee does not fall below the prescribed ceiling of P5,000.00; and
Direct those in-charge of payroll preparation to exercise diligence in monitoring the net take home pay of each personnel to comply with Section 56 of the General Provisions of Republic Act 11975 or the General Appropriations Act for Fiscal Year 2024.

We have recommended that the Management:  

Comply with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 by ensuring that the members of the Inventory Committee are temporarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed;

Submit the required Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment showing relevant information, such as property number, unit value, balance of PPE per property cards and per count; and

Undertake all other procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 
2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer prepare and maintain Property/Ledger Cards for all properties as required under Section 114 of the Manual to provide adequate records about the property, plant and equipment of the municipality and provide sufficient basis for the correct provision of depreciation periodically. Likewise, the Committee on Inventory, Appraisal and Disposal Committee to observe the provisions of Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGU regarding the prescribed timeline on the conduct of physical inventory and the submission of the RPCPPE to the Audit Team to allow the latter to make the necessary verification and reconciliation.

We recommended that Management take proper action on government properties that are for disposal. For those properties found to be valuable, cause the immediate sale in accordance with the guidelines set forth by Section 79 of PD 1445 and Item V of COA Circular No. 92-386 to prevent their further deterioration and loss of additional income that could have been accrued to the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact all her collections as well as collections turned over to her by the collectors with authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day pursuant Section 69 (1) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) 1445 and/or Section 307 of RA 7160 otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991 to avoid possible misuse of the municipal funds.

We recommended that only bonded accountable officer shall perform the collection function and be issued accountable forms pursuant Section 101 of PD 1445 and Par. 4.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 to protect public funds against possible loss.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile her cashbook balances regularly with the Accounting Office as provided for by Book 2 Chapter 2 Section 63 of Local Treasury Operation Manual of Bureau of Local Government Finance for a strong internal control system of the municipality. Moreover, the Municipal Treasurer should give her written explanation for the non-reconciliation of cashbooks balances.
We recommended that the Collectors and other Local Accountable Officers to maintain and use cashbooks of collections and remittance for a strong internal control system of the municipality.

We recommended that concerned personnel submit the financial reports of the Municipality within the deadline set by the Commission so that management will be informed early of any defects and/or deficiencies noted in the audit pursuant to Section 122 of Presidential Decree 1445 and Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant disclosed in financial statement the amount due to be settled after twelve months from the reporting date after the to present fairly the financial statements of the Agency and in accordance with PPSAS I.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management formulate a Policy Guideline for contractual/casual/job order employees, taking into consideration the necessity of hiring and the reasonable number of contractual/casual/job order employees to be assigned in different offices in order to avoid excessive manpower and work duplication.  

The policy guidelines to be formulated shall not, in any way, restrict the manpower requirements needed in the delivery of vital services for the implementation of the various programs and activities of the municipality and will promote optimum utilization of financial resources.
observations.managementAction: Management has formulated a Policy Guideline for contractual/ casual/job order employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that public bidding shall be conducted in the procurement of goods in accordance with the revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in order to avail the lowest price advantageous to the government and adhere to the principle of transparency, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in its procurement process. We also recommended that alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in the highly exceptional cases of procurement.
observations.managementAction: The procurement of goods and services by the municipality is in accordance with the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.
